1	/**
2	 * API client for the Kanban board REST API.
3	 * All functions use relative URLs so Vite's dev proxy (/api → localhost:3001)
4	 * and same-origin production serving both work without configuration.
5	 */
6	
7	// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
8	// Type definitions
9	// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
10	
11	/**
12	 * @typedef {Object} Card
13	 * @property {string} id
14	 * @property {string} title
15	 * @property {string|null} assignee
16	 * @property {string} column - e.g. 'ready', 'in-progress', 'done'
17	 * @property {number} position
18	 * @property {string|null} description
19	 * @property {number} created_at - Unix ms timestamp
20	 * @property {Comment[]} comments
21	 */
22	
23	/**
24	 * @typedef {Object} Comment
25	 * @property {string} id
26	 * @property {string} card_id
27	 * @property {string} author
28	 * @property {string} content
29	 * @property {number} created_at
30	 */
31	
32	// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
33	// ApiError
34	// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
35	
36	export class ApiError extends Error {
37	  /**
38	   * @param {string} msg
39	   * @param {number} status  HTTP status code, or 0 for network-level failures
40	   * @param {*} data         Parsed error response body, or null
41	   */
42	  constructor(msg, status, data) {
43	    super(msg)
44	    this.name = 'ApiError'
45	    this.status = status
46	    this.data = data
47	  }
48	}
49	
50	// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
51	// Internal fetch helper
52	// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
53	
54	async function apiFetch(path, options = {}) {
55	  const headers = {}
56	  if (options.body !== undefined) headers['Content-Type'] = 'application/json'
57	
58	  let response
59	  try {
60	    response = await fetch(path, { ...options, headers: { ...headers, ...options.headers } })
61	  } catch (err) {
62	    throw new ApiError(`Network error: ${err.message}`, 0, null)
63	  }
64	
65	  if (!response.ok) {
66	    let errorData = null
67	    try { errorData = await response.json() } catch { /* ignore */ }
68	    const message = errorData?.error ?? `HTTP error ${response.status}`
69	    throw new ApiError(message, response.status, errorData)
70	  }
71	
72	  if (response.status === 204 || response.headers.get('content-length') === '0') {
73	    return null
74	  }
75	
76	  try {
77	    return await response.json()
78	  } catch (err) {
79	    throw new ApiError(`Failed to parse response: ${err.message}`, response.status, null)
80	  }
81	}
82	
83	// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
84	// Public API functions
85	// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
86	
87	/**
88	 * Fetch all cards with their nested comments.
89	 * @returns {Promise<Card[]>}
90	 * @throws {ApiError} on network or HTTP error
91	 * @example
92	 * const cards = await fetchCards()
93	 * console.log(cards[0].title)
94	 */
95	export async function fetchCards() {
96	  return apiFetch('/api/cards')
97	}
98	
99	/**
100	 * Create a new card.
101	 * @param {{ title: string, assignee?: string, column?: string, description?: string }} data
102	 * @returns {Promise<Card>}
103	 * @throws {ApiError} on network or HTTP error
104	 * @example
105	 * const card = await createCard({ title: 'Fix bug', assignee: 'Alice' })
106	 * console.log(card.id)
107	 */
108	export async function createCard(data) {
109	  return apiFetch('/api/cards', { method: 'POST', body: JSON.stringify(data) })
110	}
111	
112	/**
113	 * Partially update an existing card.
114	 * @param {string} id
115	 * @param {{ title?: string, assignee?: string, column?: string, description?: string, position?: number }} data
116	 * @returns {Promise<Card>}
117	 * @throws {ApiError} on network or HTTP error
118	 * @example
119	 * const updated = await updateCard('abc-123', { column: 'done' })
120	 */
121	export async function updateCard(id, data) {
122	  return apiFetch(`/api/cards/${id}`, { method: 'PATCH', body: JSON.stringify(data) })
123	}
124	
125	/**
126	 * Delete a card and all its comments.
127	 * @param {string} id
128	 * @returns {Promise<null>}
129	 * @throws {ApiError} on network or HTTP error
130	 * @example
131	 * await deleteCard('abc-123')
132	 */
133	export async function deleteCard(id) {
134	  return apiFetch(`/api/cards/${id}`, { method: 'DELETE' })
135	}
136	
137	/**
138	 * Add a comment to a card.
139	 * @param {string} cardId
140	 * @param {{ author: string, content: string }} data
141	 * @returns {Promise<Comment>}
142	 * @throws {ApiError} on network or HTTP error
143	 * @example
144	 * const comment = await createComment('abc-123', { author: 'Bob', content: 'Looks good!' })
145	 */
146	export async function createComment(cardId, data) {
147	  return apiFetch(`/api/cards/${cardId}/comments`, { method: 'POST', body: JSON.stringify(data) })
148	}

1	import { useState, useEffect, useCallback, useRef } from 'react'

5	  updateCard as apiUpdateCard,

11	// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
12	// Module-level helpers
13	// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
14	
15	/**
16	 * Map API column value → state key.
17	 * 'in-progress' (hyphen) → 'in_progress' (underscore); others pass through.
18	 * @param {string} col
19	 * @returns {string}
20	 */
21	export function columnToKey(col) {
22	  return col === 'in-progress' ? 'in_progress' : col
23	}
24	
25	/**
26	 * Group a flat array of cards into the board state shape, sorted by position.
27	 * @param {import('../api/client.js').Card[]} cards
28	 * @returns {{ ready: Card[], in_progress: Card[], done: Card[] }}
29	 */
30	function groupCards(cards) {
31	  const board = { ready: [], in_progress: [], done: [] }
32	  for (const card of cards) {
33	    const key = columnToKey(card.column)
34	    if (key in board) board[key].push(card)
35	  }
36	  for (const key of Object.keys(board)) {
37	    board[key].sort((a, b) => a.position - b.position)
38	  }
39	  return board
40	}
41	
42	const EMPTY_BOARD = { ready: [], in_progress: [], done: [] }

44	// Temp-ID counter for optimistic records (module-level so it's stable across renders)

56	// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
57	
58	/**
59	 * Manages board state: cards grouped by column, loading, and error.
60	 *
61	 * Column convention
62	 *   - State keys:  ready | in_progress | done  (underscore)
63	 *   - API values:  ready | in-progress  | done  (hyphen for in-progress)
64	 *   - All hook functions that accept a column name expect the API format.
65	 *   - Each card's .column field retains the API format.
66	 *
67	 * @returns {{
68	 *   cards: { ready: Card[], in_progress: Card[], done: Card[] },
69	 *   loading: boolean,
70	 *   error: string|null,
71	 *   createCard: (data: object) => Promise<Card>,
72	 *   updateCard: (id: string, data: object) => Promise<Card>,
73	 *   deleteCard: (id: string) => Promise<void>,
74	 *   moveCard: (id: string, targetColumn: string, position?: number) => Promise<Card>,
75	 *   addComment: (cardId: string, data: object) => Promise<Comment>,

78	export function useBoard() {

91	  const cardsRef = useRef(EMPTY_BOARD)

103	   */
104	  function applyCards(updater) {

106	    cardsRef.current = next

111	  function endOp()   { if (--pendingRef.current === 0) setLoading(false) }

118	    const expiry = suppressedRef.current.get(id)
119	    if (expiry === undefined) return false
120	    suppressedRef.current.delete(id)
121	    return Date.now() <= expiry
122	  }
123	
124	  // -------------------------------------------------------------------------
125	  // WebSocket event handler (stub — filled in Subtask 2)
126	  // -------------------------------------------------------------------------
127	
128	  // useCallback([]) is safe: applyCards/consumeSuppression close only over stable refs
129	  const handleWsEvent = useCallback((eventType, payload) => {
130	    switch (eventType) {
131	      case 'card:created': {
132	        if (consumeSuppression(payload.id)) break
133	        const key = columnToKey(payload.column)
134	        applyCards(prev => {
135	          if (prev[key].some(c => c.id === payload.id)) return prev
136	          return {
137	            ...prev,
138	            [key]: [...prev[key], { ...payload, comments: payload.comments ?? [] }]
139	              .sort((a, b) => a.position - b.position),
140	          }
141	        })
142	        break
143	      }
144	      case 'card:updated': {
145	        if (consumeSuppression(payload.id)) break
146	        // Remove from current column, re-insert in correct column.
147	        // Handles both in-place updates AND cross-column moves via PATCH /api/cards/:id.
148	        // Preserves existing comments (WS payload does not include comments array).
149	        const newKey = columnToKey(payload.column)
150	        applyCards(prev => {
151	          let existingComments = []
152	          let found = false
153	          const next = {}
154	          for (const [k, col] of Object.entries(prev)) {
155	            const card = col.find(c => c.id === payload.id)
156	            if (card) { existingComments = card.comments ?? []; found = true }
157	            next[k] = col.filter(c => c.id !== payload.id)
158	          }
159	          if (!found) return prev
160	          next[newKey] = [...next[newKey], { ...payload, comments: existingComments }]
161	            .sort((a, b) => a.position - b.position)
162	          return next
163	        })
164	        break
165	      }
166	      case 'card:deleted': {
167	        if (consumeSuppression(payload.id)) break
168	        applyCards(prev => {
169	          const next = {}
170	          for (const [k, col] of Object.entries(prev)) {
171	            next[k] = col.filter(c => c.id !== payload.id)
172	          }
173	          return next
174	        })
175	        break
176	      }
177	      case 'card:moved': {
178	        if (consumeSuppression(payload.id)) break
179	        const newKey = columnToKey(payload.column)
180	        applyCards(prev => {
181	          const next = {}
182	          let existingComments = []
183	          let found = false
184	          for (const [k, col] of Object.entries(prev)) {
185	            const card = col.find(c => c.id === payload.id)
186	            if (card) { existingComments = card.comments ?? []; found = true }
187	            next[k] = col.filter(c => c.id !== payload.id)
188	          }
189	          if (!found) return prev
190	          next[newKey] = [...next[newKey], { ...payload, comments: existingComments }]
191	            .sort((a, b) => a.position - b.position)
192	          return next
193	        })
194	        break
195	      }
196	      case 'comment:created': {
197	        if (consumeSuppression(payload.id)) break
198	        applyCards(prev => {
199	          const next = {}
200	          for (const [k, col] of Object.entries(prev)) {
201	            next[k] = col.map(c =>
202	              c.id === payload.card_id
203	                ? { ...c, comments: [...c.comments, payload] }
204	                : c
205	            )
206	          }
207	          return next
208	        })
209	        break
210	      }
211	    }
212	  }, [])
213	
214	  const { status: wsStatus } = useWebSocket(WS_URL, { onEvent: handleWsEvent, events: WS_EVENTS })
215	
216	  // -------------------------------------------------------------------------
217	  // Reconnect reconciliation — re-fetch on WS reconnect to recover missed events
218	  // -------------------------------------------------------------------------
219	
220	  useEffect(() => {
221	    if (wsStatus === 'connected') {
222	      if (hasConnectedRef.current) {
223	        // Reconnect detected — re-fetch to reconcile any missed events
224	        let cancelled = false
225	        fetchCards()
226	          .then(data => { if (!cancelled) applyCards(groupCards(data)) })
227	          .catch(() => {}) // silent fail; don't disrupt existing state
228	        return () => { cancelled = true }
229	      }
230	      hasConnectedRef.current = true
231	    }
232	  }, [wsStatus])
233	
234	  // -------------------------------------------------------------------------
235	  // Initial data load
236	  // -------------------------------------------------------------------------
237	
238	  useEffect(() => {
239	    let cancelled = false
240	    setError(null)
241	    beginOp()
242	
243	    fetchCards()
244	      .then(data => {
245	        if (cancelled) return
246	        applyCards(groupCards(data))
247	        endOp()
248	      })
249	      .catch(err => {
250	        if (cancelled) return
251	        setError(err.message)
252	        endOp()
253	      })
254	
255	    return () => { cancelled = true }
256	  }, [])
257	
258	  // -------------------------------------------------------------------------
259	  // createCard — temp-ID optimistic pattern
260	  // -------------------------------------------------------------------------
261	
262	  const createCard = useCallback(async (data) => {
263	    const tempId = nextTempId()
264	    const col = data.column ?? 'ready'
265	    const key = columnToKey(col)
266	
267	    const optimisticCard = {
268	      id: tempId,
269	      title: data.title,
270	      assignee: data.assignee ?? null,
271	      column: col,
272	      position: Infinity,
273	      description: data.description ?? null,
274	      created_at: Date.now(),
275	      comments: [],
276	    }
277	
278	    applyCards(prev => ({ ...prev, [key]: [...prev[key], optimisticCard] }))
279	    beginOp()
280	
281	    try {
282	      const created = await apiCreateCard(data)
283	      suppressWsEvent(created.id)
284	      const createdKey = columnToKey(created.column)
285	      applyCards(prev => {
286	        const next = { ...prev }
287	        // Remove the temp card from whichever column it was placed in
288	        next[key] = next[key].filter(c => c.id !== tempId)
289	        // Insert the server card into the column the server assigned
290	        next[createdKey] = [...next[createdKey], created]
291	          .sort((a, b) => a.position - b.position)
292	        return next
293	      })
294	      return created
295	    } catch (err) {
296	      applyCards(prev => ({
297	        ...prev,
298	        [key]: prev[key].filter(c => c.id !== tempId),
299	      }))
300	      throw err
301	    } finally {
302	      endOp()
303	    }
304	  }, [])
305	
306	  // -------------------------------------------------------------------------
307	  // updateCard — rollback pattern (column changes ignored; use moveCard)
308	  // -------------------------------------------------------------------------
309	
310	  const updateCard = useCallback(async (id, data) => {
311	    // Strip column — column changes must go through moveCard
312	    const safeData = { ...data }
313	    delete safeData.column
314	
315	    // Capture rollback synchronously from the ref (not inside a lazy updater)
316	    const rollback = cardsRef.current
317	    suppressWsEvent(id)
318	    applyCards(prev => {
319	      const next = {}
320	      for (const [k, col] of Object.entries(prev)) {
321	        next[k] = col.map(c => c.id === id ? { ...c, ...safeData } : c)
322	      }
323	      return next
324	    })
325	    beginOp()
326	
327	    try {
328	      const updated = await apiUpdateCard(id, safeData)
329	      applyCards(prev => {
330	        const next = {}
331	        for (const [k, col] of Object.entries(prev)) {
332	          next[k] = col.map(c => c.id === id ? updated : c)
333	        }
334	        return next
335	      })
336	      return updated
337	    } catch (err) {
338	      applyCards(rollback)
339	      throw err
340	    } finally {
341	      endOp()
342	    }
343	  }, [])
344	
345	  // -------------------------------------------------------------------------
346	  // deleteCard — rollback pattern
347	  // -------------------------------------------------------------------------
348	
349	  const deleteCard = useCallback(async (id) => {
350	    // Capture rollback synchronously from the ref
351	    const rollback = cardsRef.current
352	    suppressWsEvent(id)
353	    applyCards(prev => {
354	      const next = {}
355	      for (const [k, col] of Object.entries(prev)) {
356	        next[k] = col.filter(c => c.id !== id)
357	      }
358	      return next
359	    })
360	    beginOp()
361	
362	    try {
363	      await apiDeleteCard(id)
364	    } catch (err) {
365	      applyCards(rollback)
366	      throw err
367	    } finally {
368	      endOp()
369	    }
370	  }, [])
371	
372	  // -------------------------------------------------------------------------
373	  // moveCard — rollback pattern
374	  // targetColumn must be in API format ('ready' | 'in-progress' | 'done')
375	  // -------------------------------------------------------------------------
376	
377	  const moveCard = useCallback(async (id, targetColumn, position) => {
378	    const targetKey = columnToKey(targetColumn)
379	
380	    // Capture rollback synchronously from the ref
381	    const rollback = cardsRef.current
382	    suppressWsEvent(id)
383	    applyCards(prev => {
384	      let cardToMove = null
385	      const next = {}
386	
387	      for (const [k, col] of Object.entries(prev)) {
388	        const idx = col.findIndex(c => c.id === id)
389	        if (idx !== -1) {
390	          cardToMove = col[idx]
391	          next[k] = col.filter(c => c.id !== id)
392	        } else {
393	          next[k] = col
394	        }
395	      }
396	
397	      if (!cardToMove) return prev
398	
399	      const movedCard = {
400	        ...cardToMove,
401	        column: targetColumn,
402	        ...(position !== undefined ? { position } : {}),
403	      }
404	      next[targetKey] = [...next[targetKey], movedCard]
405	        .sort((a, b) => a.position - b.position)
406	      return next
407	    })
408	    beginOp()
409	
410	    try {
411	      const payload = { column: targetColumn }
412	      if (position !== undefined) payload.position = position
413	      const updated = await apiUpdateCard(id, payload)
414	      const updatedKey = columnToKey(updated.column)
415	      applyCards(prev => {
416	        const next = {}
417	        for (const [k, col] of Object.entries(prev)) {
418	          next[k] = col.filter(c => c.id !== id)
419	        }
420	        next[updatedKey] = [...next[updatedKey], updated]
421	          .sort((a, b) => a.position - b.position)
422	        return next
423	      })
424	      return updated
425	    } catch (err) {
426	      applyCards(rollback)
427	      throw err
428	    } finally {
429	      endOp()
430	    }
431	  }, [])
432	
433	  // -------------------------------------------------------------------------
434	  // addComment — temp-ID optimistic pattern
435	  // -------------------------------------------------------------------------
436	
437	  const addComment = useCallback(async (cardId, data) => {
438	    const tempId = nextTempId()
439	    const optimisticComment = {
440	      id: tempId,
441	      card_id: cardId,
442	      author: data.author,
443	      content: data.content,
444	      created_at: Date.now(),
445	    }
446	
447	    applyCards(prev => {
448	      const next = {}
449	      for (const [k, col] of Object.entries(prev)) {
450	        next[k] = col.map(c =>
451	          c.id === cardId
452	            ? { ...c, comments: [...c.comments, optimisticComment] }
453	            : c
454	        )
455	      }
456	      return next
457	    })
458	    beginOp()
459	
460	    try {
461	      const comment = await createComment(cardId, data)
462	      suppressWsEvent(comment.id)
463	      applyCards(prev => {
464	        const next = {}
465	        for (const [k, col] of Object.entries(prev)) {
466	          next[k] = col.map(c =>
467	            c.id === cardId
468	              ? {
469	                  ...c,
470	                  comments: c.comments
471	                    .filter(cm => cm.id !== tempId)
472	                    .concat(comment),
473	                }
474	              : c
475	          )
476	        }
477	        return next
478	      })
479	      return comment
480	    } catch (err) {
481	      applyCards(prev => {
482	        const next = {}
483	        for (const [k, col] of Object.entries(prev)) {
484	          next[k] = col.map(c =>
485	            c.id === cardId
486	              ? { ...c, comments: c.comments.filter(cm => cm.id !== tempId) }
487	              : c
488	          )
489	        }
490	        return next
491	      })
492	      throw err
493	    } finally {
494	      endOp()
495	    }
496	  }, [])
497	
498	  // -------------------------------------------------------------------------
499	  // Public interface

512	}

# QA Report — Task 22: Add Inline Editing Functionality **Date:** 2026-04-10 **Branch:** `kanban-board/kanban-board-22` **Reviewer role:** QA Engineer (read-only, no fixes applied) --- ## 1. Scope This report covers the inline editing implementation introduced in commit `e1d4024`: - `kanban/client/src/components/Board/CardTile.jsx` — click-to-edit for title and assignee on the card tile - `kanban/client/src/components/Board/CardModal.jsx` — inline editing (title/assignee) inside the card modal - `kanban/client/src/components/Board/CardTile.css` — styling for edit state - `kanban/client/src/components/Board/CardModal.css` — styling for modal edit fields - `kanban/client/src/components/Board/CardTile.test.jsx` — 37 new inline-editing tests added - `kanban/client/src/components/Board/CardModal.test.jsx` — 68 tests (full rewrite to cover inline editing) - `kanban/client/src/components/Board/Column.jsx` — `onUpdate` prop now threaded through to `CardTile` - `kanban/client/src/components/Board/Board.jsx` — `onUpdate={updateCard}` now passed to all `Column` instances --- ## 2. Commands Found | Command | Location | Purpose | |---------|----------|---------| | `npm -w client run lint` | `kanban/package.json` via workspace | ESLint on `src/` | | `npm -w client run test` | `kanban/package.json` via workspace | Vitest (client unit tests) | | `npm -w client run build` | `kanban/package.json` via workspace | Vite production build | | `npm -w server run test` | `kanban/package.json` via workspace | Node built-in test runner (server unit tests) | | `node --test test/*.test.mjs` | `kanban/package.json` (`test:setup`) | Setup/integration tests at the root test/ directory | All commands were run from `/Users/benjamin/.sofactory/worktrees/kanban-board/kanban-board-22/kanban`. --- ## 3. Command Results ### 3.1 Lint — `npm -w client run lint` **Result: PASS** ESLint completed with no errors or warnings. Exit code 0. ``` > kanban-client@1.0.0 lint > eslint src ``` --- ### 3.2 Client Tests — `npm -w client run test` **Result: PARTIAL FAIL (pre-existing failures unrelated to task 22)** ``` Test Files 3 failed | 8 passed (11) Tests 349 passed (349) ``` **Passing test suites (all task-22-relevant suites pass):** | Suite | Tests | |-------|-------| | `src/api/client.test.js` | 27 | | `src/hooks/useWebSocket.test.js` | 42 | | `src/components/CardModal/CommentList.test.jsx` | 32 | | `src/components/Board/CardTile.test.jsx` | **38** | | `src/components/CreateCardForm.test.jsx` | 33 | | `src/components/Board/Column.test.jsx` | 8 | | `src/components/Board/CardModal.test.jsx` | **68** | | `src/hooks/useBoard.test.js` | 101 | All 349 individual tests pass. **Failing suites (3, all pre-existing — caused by `@blocknote` packages not installed in `node_modules`):** - `src/App.test.jsx` — cannot resolve `@blocknote/core` - `src/components/Board/Board.test.jsx` — cannot resolve `@blocknote/core` - `src/components/CardModal/BlockEditor.test.jsx` — cannot resolve `@blocknote/core` **Confirmed pre-existing:** These 3 suites also failed before task 22 (verified by checking out `HEAD~1` — `f0bd015`, the Docker configuration commit — which produced the same 3 failures with 312 tests passing instead of 349). The `@blocknote` packages are listed as `dependencies` in `client/package.json` but are absent from `node_modules`. This was introduced in task 14 and is out of scope for this review. --- ### 3.3 Client Build — `npm -w client run build` **Result: FAIL** ``` error during build: [vite]: Rollup failed to resolve import "@blocknote/core" from ".../kanban/client/src/components/CardModal/BlockEditor.jsx". ``` **Pre-existing failure.** The `@blocknote/core` and `@blocknote/react` packages are missing from `node_modules`. This prevents production bundling. Same root cause as the 3 failing test suites above; introduced in task 14, not task 22. --- ### 3.4 Server Tests — `npm -w server run test` **Result: FAIL (pre-existing)** ``` # tests 25 # pass 21 # fail 4 ``` Failing suites: - `test/cards.test.mjs` — `ERR_MODULE_NOT_FOUND: Cannot find package 'express'` - `test/comments.test.mjs` — `ERR_MODULE_NOT_FOUND: Cannot find package 'express'` - `test/db.test.mjs` — `ERR_MODULE_NOT_FOUND: Cannot find package 'better-sqlite3'` - `test/server.test.mjs` — `ERR_MODULE_NOT_FOUND: Cannot find package 'express'` The WebSocket-only suite (`ws.test.mjs`) passes with 21 tests because it does not depend on express or better-sqlite3. **Confirmed pre-existing:** Running the server test suite against `HEAD~1` (task 20) produces identical results. The server workspace `node_modules` is not installed (only the root workspace `node_modules` exists). This is an environment issue and not related to task 22. --- ### 3.5 Setup Tests — `node --test test/*.test.mjs` **Result: PASS** ``` # tests 76 # suites 13 # pass 76 # fail 0 ``` All 76 setup/integration tests pass (package.json shape, Docker config, file structure, etc.). --- ## 4. Implementation Review ### 4.1 CardTile — Inline Editing **File:** `kanban/client/src/components/Board/CardTile.jsx` The implementation is largely correct and matches the task requirements: - Click on title `

` enters title edit mode; click on assignee `

` enters assignee edit mode. - `stopPropagation()` on both elements prevents the card-level `onCardClick` from firing. - Mutual exclusivity: opening one field's edit mode closes the other. - `skipBlurRef` flag correctly prevents double-save when Enter key triggers save (Enter → keyDown handler fires → sets flag → blur fires → flag checked and cleared). - Enter saves; Escape cancels and restores original value. - Blur auto-saves. - Empty/whitespace-only title: `editTitle.trim() === ''` check shows error and returns early without calling `onUpdate`. - Assignee can be cleared (empty string maps to `null` in the API call). - `isSaving` state shows a "Saving…" indicator. - Error message displayed in `role="alert"` paragraph. - `card-tile-editing` CSS class applied while in any edit mode. - `useEffect` auto-focuses the active input after state update. **Issue found (behavioral inconsistency):** On title save failure in `CardTile`, the component: 1. Rolls back `editTitle` to `card.title` 2. Exits edit mode (`setIsEditingTitle(false)`) 3. Shows `saveError` This means the user loses their typed-but-unsaved title on a network error and the input disappears. The test at line 263–271 (`CardTile.test.jsx`) asserts this behavior (exits edit mode and shows original title on error), so it is intentional and tested — but it is a UX trade-off to note. By contrast, in `CardModal`, on title save failure the component **stays in edit mode** (line 65–68: only `setSaveError(err.message)` is called, no `setIsEditingTitle(false)`). This allows the user to retry or correct the value. The test at line 188–195 (`CardModal.test.jsx`) asserts "Save stays in edit mode on rejection." This is a deliberate behavioral difference between the two components but the asymmetry could be surprising. --- ### 4.2 CardModal — Inline Editing **File:** `kanban/client/src/components/Board/CardModal.jsx` The implementation is correct and well-structured: - "Edit" button triggers edit mode for title and assignee fields (separate buttons). - Save and Cancel buttons shown in edit mode with `onMouseDown` guard on both to set `skipBlurRef.current = true`, preventing the blur handler from firing a second save when clicking Save, or an unwanted save when clicking Cancel. - `handleSaveTitle`: validates non-empty title before calling `onUpdate`; shows error alert; stays in edit mode on failure (allowing retry); exits on success. - `handleSaveAssignee`: allows empty (maps to `null`); exits on success; stays in edit mode on failure. - Escape key is handled via `document.addEventListener` in a `useEffect` that intercepts before closing the modal — if an edit field is active, Escape cancels the edit (not the modal). - Mutual exclusivity between title and assignee edit modes. - `skipBlurRef` shared between title and assignee handlers — this is safe because only one field can be in edit mode at a time. - `onEditingChange` callback passed to `BlockEditor` to track description editing state, preventing Escape from closing the modal while the block editor is active. - No local rollback of the `editTitle`/`editAssignee` state on save failure (stays in edit mode with current value, which is correct — the user's input is preserved for retry). **Note:** `handleSaveTitle` does not call `setSaveError(null)` before setting `isSaving(true)` — it does call `setSaveError(null)` in the sequence at line 61: `setIsSaving(true); setSaveError(null)`. Actually this is correct; both are called. --- ### 4.3 Column / Board Wiring **File:** `kanban/client/src/components/Board/Column.jsx` `onUpdate` prop is correctly forwarded to `CardTile`. The `Board.jsx` now passes `onUpdate={updateCard}` to all three `Column` instances, including "In Progress" and "Done" which previously omitted it. This is correct. --- ### 4.4 Test Coverage Task 22 added: - **37 new tests** in `CardTile.test.jsx` (38 total; 1 pre-existing) covering all required behaviors: edit mode entry, mutual exclusivity, Enter/Escape/blur, validation, error handling, rollback, saving indicator, CSS class. - **68 tests** in `CardModal.test.jsx` (complete rewrite) covering all modal-specific inline editing behaviors plus the original modal behaviors (portal, close button, Escape, overlay click, delete confirmation). All 349 currently runnable tests pass. --- ## 5. Overall Assessment **The task 22 implementation is functionally correct and well-tested.** ### Summary | Category | Status | Notes | |----------|--------|-------| | Lint | PASS | No ESLint errors | | Client unit tests (task-relevant) | PASS | 349/349 individual tests pass | | Client build | FAIL | Pre-existing: `@blocknote` not installed (task 14 issue) | | Server unit tests | FAIL | Pre-existing: server `node_modules` not installed | | Setup tests | PASS | 76/76 pass | | Inline editing (CardTile) | PASS | All behaviors correctly implemented | | Inline editing (CardModal) | PASS | All behaviors correctly implemented | | Try-catch / error messages | PASS | Both components wrap API calls in try-catch and show `role="alert"` errors | | Rollback mechanism | PARTIAL | CardTile rolls back and exits edit mode on failure; CardModal stays in edit mode (different behavior, both tested and intentional) | | Loading states | PASS | `isSaving` state with Saving… indicator in both components | | Empty title validation | PASS | `trim() === ''` check before API call | | Enter / Escape / blur | PASS | All three interaction modes work in both components | ### Issues Found 1. **Build broken (pre-existing, not task 22):** `@blocknote` packages missing from `node_modules`. The production build fails and 3 test suites cannot load. This must be fixed before deployment. 2. **Server dependency installation (pre-existing, not task 22):** Server workspace `node_modules` missing; 4 of 5 server test suites fail. 3. **Behavioral inconsistency between CardTile and CardModal on save failure:** In `CardTile`, a failed title save exits edit mode and rolls back the displayed value. In `CardModal`, a failed title save keeps the user in edit mode with their typed value preserved. Both behaviors are tested and intentional, but the inconsistency could confuse users who experience an error in the tile (edit mode disappears) vs. the modal (edit mode remains). 4. **`skipBlurRef` is shared between title and assignee in both components:** Since only one field can be in edit mode at a time this works correctly, but it creates a subtle coupling. If the mutual-exclusivity logic were ever loosened, this shared flag would be a bug source. No regressions were introduced by task 22. All new functionality is covered by tests that pass.

By contrast, in `CardModal`, a failed title save keeps the user in edit mode with their typed value preserved, allowing retry. This asymmetry between the two editing surfaces is a potential UX inconsistency. --- ### 4.2 CardModal — Inline Editing **File:** `kanban/client/src/components/Board/CardModal.jsx` The implementation is correct and well-structured: - "Edit" button triggers edit mode for title and assignee fields (separate buttons). - Save and Cancel buttons shown in edit mode. Both behaviors are tested and intentional but asymmetric. | | Loading states | PASS | `isSaving` state with "Saving…" indicator in both components | | Empty title validation | PASS | `trim() === ''` check before API call in both components | | Enter / Escape / blur | PASS | All three interaction patterns work correctly in both components | ### Issues Found 1. **Build broken (pre-existing, not task 22):** `@blocknote` packages missing from `node_modules`. In `CardModal`, a failed save keeps the user in edit mode with their value preserved. Both behaviors are intentional and tested, but the inconsistency within the same application could be confusing to end users. 4. **Shared `skipBlurRef` between title and assignee in both components:** The flag is reused across both fields. This is safe given the mutual-exclusivity constraint, but represents a latent coupling — if mutual exclusivity were removed in a future change, the shared flag would cause incorrect blur-suppression behavior.

` enters assignee edit mode. 148 - `stopPropagation()` on both elements prevents the card-level `onCardClick` from firing. 149 - Mutual exclusivity: opening one field's edit mode closes the other. 150 - `skipBlurRef` flag correctly prevents double-save when Enter key triggers save (Enter keyDown handler sets flag, blur fires next, flag is checked and cleared without triggering a second save). 151 - Enter saves; Escape cancels and restores original value. 152 - Blur auto-saves. 153 - Empty/whitespace-only title: `editTitle.trim() === ''` check shows error and returns early without calling `onUpdate`. 154 - Assignee can be cleared (empty string maps to `null` in the API call). 155 - `isSaving` state shows a "Saving…" span with `aria-label="Saving"`. 156 - Error message displayed in `role="alert"` paragraph. 157 - `card-tile-editing` CSS class applied while in any edit mode. 158 - `useEffect` auto-focuses the active input after state update. 159 160 **Issue found (behavioral inconsistency with CardModal):** 161 162 On title save failure in `CardTile`, the component: 163 1. Rolls back `editTitle` to `card.title` 164 2. Exits edit mode (`setIsEditingTitle(false)`) 165 3. Shows `saveError` 166 167 This means the user loses their typed-but-unsaved title on a network error. The test at line 263–271 (`CardTile.test.jsx`) asserts this behavior intentionally, so it is tested — but it is a UX trade-off (the user must re-enter their change after an error). 168 169 By contrast, in `CardModal`, a failed title save keeps the user in edit mode with their typed value preserved, allowing retry. This asymmetry between the two editing surfaces is a potential UX inconsistency. 170 171 --- 172 173 ### 4.2 CardModal — Inline Editing 174 175 **File:** `kanban/client/src/components/Board/CardModal.jsx` 176 177 The implementation is correct and well-structured: 178 179 - "Edit" button triggers edit mode for title and assignee fields (separate buttons). 180 - Save and Cancel buttons shown in edit mode. Both buttons use `onMouseDown` to set `skipBlurRef.current = true`, preventing the blur handler from firing a second save when Save is clicked, or an unwanted save when Cancel is clicked. 181 - `handleSaveTitle`: validates non-empty title before calling `onUpdate`; shows error alert; stays in edit mode on failure (preserving the user's input for retry); exits on success. 182 - `handleSaveAssignee`: allows empty string (maps to `null`); exits on success; stays in edit mode on failure. 183 - Escape key handled via `document.addEventListener` in a `useEffect`: if an edit field is active, Escape cancels the edit instead of closing the modal. 184 - Mutual exclusivity between title and assignee edit modes. 185 - `skipBlurRef` shared between title and assignee handlers — safe since only one field can be in edit mode at a time. 186 - `onEditingChange` callback passed to `BlockEditor` to track description editing state, preventing Escape from closing the modal while the block editor is active. 187 188 --- 189 190 ### 4.3 Column / Board Wiring 191 192 **File:** `kanban/client/src/components/Board/Column.jsx` and `Board.jsx` 193 194 `onUpdate` prop is correctly forwarded from `Column` to each `CardTile`. `Board.jsx` now passes `onUpdate={updateCard}` to all three `Column` instances (Ready, In Progress, Done). This is correct; previously In Progress and Done omitted this prop. 195 196 --- 197 198 ### 4.4 Test Coverage 199 200 Task 22 added: 201 - **37 new tests** in `CardTile.test.jsx` (38 total; 1 pre-existing) covering all required behaviors: edit mode entry, mutual exclusivity, Enter/Escape/blur, validation, error handling, rollback, saving indicator, CSS class. 202 - **68 tests** in `CardModal.test.jsx` (complete rewrite) covering all modal-specific inline editing behaviors plus the original modal behaviors (portal, close button, Escape, overlay click, delete confirmation). 203 204 All 349 currently runnable tests pass. 205 206 --- 207 208 ## 5. Overall Assessment 209 210 **The task 22 implementation is functionally correct and well-tested.** No regressions were introduced. 211 212 ### Summary Table 213 214 | Category | Status | Notes | 215 |----------|--------|-------| 216 | Lint | PASS | No ESLint errors | 217 | Client unit tests (task-relevant) | PASS | 349/349 individual tests pass | 218 | Client build | FAIL | Pre-existing: `@blocknote` not installed (task 14 issue) | 219 | Server unit tests | FAIL | Pre-existing: server `node_modules` not installed | 220 | Setup tests | PASS | 76/76 pass | 221 | Inline editing — CardTile | PASS | All required behaviors correctly implemented | 222 | Inline editing — CardModal | PASS | All required behaviors correctly implemented | 223 | Try-catch / error messages | PASS | Both components wrap API calls in try-catch and display `role="alert"` errors | 224 | Rollback mechanism | PARTIAL | CardTile rolls back and exits edit mode on failure; CardModal stays in edit mode. Both behaviors are tested and intentional but asymmetric. | 225 | Loading states | PASS | `isSaving` state with "Saving…" indicator in both components | 226 | Empty title validation | PASS | `trim() === ''` check before API call in both components | 227 | Enter / Escape / blur | PASS | All three interaction patterns work correctly in both components | 228 229 ### Issues Found 230 231 1. **Build broken (pre-existing, not task 22):** `@blocknote` packages missing from `node_modules`. The production build fails and 3 test suites cannot load. This must be resolved before deployment. 232 233 2. **Server dependency installation (pre-existing, not task 22):** Server workspace `node_modules` missing; 4 of 5 server test suites fail with `ERR_MODULE_NOT_FOUND`. 234 235 3. **Behavioral asymmetry on save failure between CardTile and CardModal:** In `CardTile`, a failed save exits edit mode and discards the user's typed value. In `CardModal`, a failed save keeps the user in edit mode with their value preserved. Both behaviors are intentional and tested, but the inconsistency within the same application could be confusing to end users. 236 237 4. **Shared `skipBlurRef` between title and assignee in both components:** The flag is reused across both fields. This is safe given the mutual-exclusivity constraint, but represents a latent coupling — if mutual exclusivity were removed in a future change, the shared flag would cause incorrect blur-suppression behavior. 238

## Summary

**Task 22 implementation is functionally correct and well-tested.** Here's the breakdown:

### ✅ Passes
| Check | Result |
|-------|--------|
| ESLint | No errors |
| Client unit tests (task-relevant) | 349/349 pass — all 37 new CardTile tests + all 68 CardModal tests |
| Setup tests | 76/76 pass |
| Inline editing behaviors (Enter/Escape/blur/validation) | Correct in both components |
| Error handling + rollback | Implemented with try-catch and `role="alert"` error messages |
| Loading states | `isSaving` indicator present in both components |

### ❌ Failures (all pre-existing, not caused by Task 22)
1. **Client build fails** — `@blocknote/core` and `@blocknote/react` packages missing from `node_modules` (introduced in task 14). Blocks production bundling and 3 test suites.
2. **Server tests fail** — `express` and `better-sqlite3` missing from server `node_modules`. 4 of 5 server test suites crash with `ERR_MODULE_NOT_FOUND`.

### ⚠️ Minor Issues Noted
1. **Behavioral asymmetry on save failure**: `CardTile` exits edit mode and discards the typed value on API error; `CardModal` stays in edit mode and preserves it. Both are tested intentionally, but the inconsistency may confuse users.
2. **Shared `skipBlurRef`** between title/assignee fields is safe now but fragile if mutual-exclusivity is ever relaxed.

1	import { render, screen, fireEvent, waitFor } from '@testing-library/react'
2	import { describe, it, expect, vi } from 'vitest'
3	import CardTile from './CardTile.jsx'
4	
5	const card = {
6	  id: '1',
7	  title: 'Fix bug',
8	  assignee: 'Alice',
9	  description: 'Short desc',
10	  column: 'ready',
11	  position: 1,
12	  created_at: Date.now(),
13	  comments: [],
14	}
15	
16	describe('CardTile', () => {
17	  it('renders card title', () => {
18	    render(<CardTile card={card} onCardClick={vi.fn()} />)
19	    expect(screen.getByText('Fix bug')).toBeInTheDocument()
20	  })
21	
22	  it('renders assignee name when provided', () => {
23	    render(<CardTile card={card} onCardClick={vi.fn()} />)
24	    expect(screen.getByText('Alice')).toBeInTheDocument()
25	  })
26	
27	  it("renders 'Unassigned' when assignee is null", () => {
28	    render(<CardTile card={{ ...card, assignee: null }} onCardClick={vi.fn()} />)
29	    expect(screen.getByText('Unassigned')).toBeInTheDocument()
30	  })
31	
32	  it('renders description when provided', () => {
33	    render(<CardTile card={card} onCardClick={vi.fn()} />)
34	    expect(screen.getByText('Short desc')).toBeInTheDocument()
35	  })
36	
37	  it('does not render description element when description is null', () => {
38	    render(<CardTile card={{ ...card, description: null }} onCardClick={vi.fn()} />)
39	    expect(screen.queryByText('Short desc')).toBeNull()
40	  })
41	
42	  it('calls onCardClick with the card object when clicked', () => {
43	    const handler = vi.fn()
44	    render(<CardTile card={card} onCardClick={handler} />)
45	    fireEvent.click(screen.getByRole('button', { name: 'Fix bug' }))
46	    expect(handler).toHaveBeenCalledWith(card)
47	  })
48	
49	  it('calls onCardClick when Enter key is pressed', () => {
50	    const handler = vi.fn()
51	    render(<CardTile card={card} onCardClick={handler} />)
52	    fireEvent.keyDown(screen.getByRole('button'), { key: 'Enter' })
53	    expect(handler).toHaveBeenCalledWith(card)
54	  })
55	
56	  it('calls onCardClick when Space key is pressed', () => {
57	    const handler = vi.fn()
58	    render(<CardTile card={card} onCardClick={handler} />)
59	    fireEvent.keyDown(screen.getByRole('button'), { key: ' ' })
60	    expect(handler).toHaveBeenCalledWith(card)
61	  })
62	
63	  it('is keyboard-focusable', () => {
64	    render(<CardTile card={card} onCardClick={vi.fn()} />)
65	    expect(screen.getByRole('button')).toHaveAttribute('tabindex', '0')
66	  })
67	})
68	
69	describe('CardTile — inline editing', () => {
70	  // Edit mode entry
71	  it('clicking title text renders a title input initialized to the card title', () => {
72	    render(<CardTile card={card} onCardClick={vi.fn()} onUpdate={vi.fn()} />)
73	    fireEvent.click(screen.getByRole('heading', { name: 'Fix bug' }))
74	    expect(screen.getByRole('textbox', { name: 'Edit title' })).toHaveValue('Fix bug')
75	  })
76	
77	  it('clicking title text does not call onCardClick', () => {
78	    const onCardClick = vi.fn()
79	    render(<CardTile card={card} onCardClick={onCardClick} onUpdate={vi.fn()} />)
80	    fireEvent.click(screen.getByRole('heading', { name: 'Fix bug' }))
81	    expect(onCardClick).not.toHaveBeenCalled()
82	  })
83	
84	  it('clicking title text focuses the title input automatically', async () => {
85	    render(<CardTile card={card} onCardClick={vi.fn()} onUpdate={vi.fn()} />)
86	    fireEvent.click(screen.getByRole('heading', { name: 'Fix bug' }))
87	    await waitFor(() => expect(screen.getByRole('textbox', { name: 'Edit title' })).toHaveFocus())
88	  })
89	
90	  it('clicking assignee text renders an assignee input initialized to the card assignee', () => {
91	    render(<CardTile card={card} onCardClick={vi.fn()} onUpdate={vi.fn()} />)
92	    fireEvent.click(screen.getByText('Alice'))
93	    expect(screen.getByRole('textbox', { name: 'Edit assignee' })).toHaveValue('Alice')
94	  })
95	
96	  it('clicking assignee text does not call onCardClick', () => {
97	    const onCardClick = vi.fn()
98	    render(<CardTile card={card} onCardClick={onCardClick} onUpdate={vi.fn()} />)
99	    fireEvent.click(screen.getByText('Alice'))
100	    expect(onCardClick).not.toHaveBeenCalled()
101	  })
102	
103	  it('clicking assignee text focuses the assignee input automatically', async () => {
104	    render(<CardTile card={card} onCardClick={vi.fn()} onUpdate={vi.fn()} />)
105	    fireEvent.click(screen.getByText('Alice'))
106	    await waitFor(() => expect(screen.getByRole('textbox', { name: 'Edit assignee' })).toHaveFocus())
107	  })
108	
109	  it('clicking assignee text renders an empty input when assignee is null', () => {
110	    render(<CardTile card={{ ...card, assignee: null }} onCardClick={vi.fn()} onUpdate={vi.fn()} />)
111	    fireEvent.click(screen.getByText('Unassigned'))
112	    expect(screen.getByRole('textbox', { name: 'Edit assignee' })).toHaveValue('')
113	  })
114	
115	  it('clicking the card body (not title/assignee) still calls onCardClick when not editing', () => {
116	    const onCardClick = vi.fn()
117	    render(<CardTile card={card} onCardClick={onCardClick} onUpdate={vi.fn()} />)
118	    fireEvent.click(screen.getByRole('button', { name: 'Fix bug' }))
119	    expect(onCardClick).toHaveBeenCalledWith(card)
120	  })
121	
122	  // Edit mode guard
123	  it('clicking the card body while in title edit mode does not call onCardClick', () => {
124	    const onCardClick = vi.fn()
125	    render(<CardTile card={card} onCardClick={onCardClick} onUpdate={vi.fn()} />)
126	    fireEvent.click(screen.getByRole('heading', { name: 'Fix bug' }))
127	    fireEvent.click(screen.getByRole('button', { name: 'Fix bug' }))
128	    expect(onCardClick).not.toHaveBeenCalled()
129	  })
130	
131	  // Mutual exclusivity
132	  it('clicking title while editing assignee closes assignee edit and opens title edit', () => {
133	    render(<CardTile card={card} onCardClick={vi.fn()} onUpdate={vi.fn()} />)
134	    fireEvent.click(screen.getByText('Alice'))
135	    expect(screen.getByRole('textbox', { name: 'Edit assignee' })).toBeInTheDocument()
136	    fireEvent.click(screen.getByRole('heading', { name: 'Fix bug' }))
137	    expect(screen.queryByRole('textbox', { name: 'Edit assignee' })).not.toBeInTheDocument()
138	    expect(screen.getByRole('textbox', { name: 'Edit title' })).toBeInTheDocument()
139	  })
140	
141	  it('clicking assignee while editing title closes title edit and opens assignee edit', () => {
142	    render(<CardTile card={card} onCardClick={vi.fn()} onUpdate={vi.fn()} />)
143	    fireEvent.click(screen.getByRole('heading', { name: 'Fix bug' }))
144	    expect(screen.getByRole('textbox', { name: 'Edit title' })).toBeInTheDocument()
145	    fireEvent.click(screen.getByText('Alice'))
146	    expect(screen.queryByRole('textbox', { name: 'Edit title' })).not.toBeInTheDocument()
147	    expect(screen.getByRole('textbox', { name: 'Edit assignee' })).toBeInTheDocument()
148	  })
149	
150	  // Enter key saves
151	  it('pressing Enter on title input calls onUpdate(id, { title }) and exits edit mode', async () => {
152	    const onUpdate = vi.fn().mockResolvedValue({})
153	    render(<CardTile card={card} onCardClick={vi.fn()} onUpdate={onUpdate} />)
154	    fireEvent.click(screen.getByRole('heading', { name: 'Fix bug' }))
155	    fireEvent.change(screen.getByRole('textbox', { name: 'Edit title' }), { target: { value: 'New title' } })
156	    fireEvent.keyDown(screen.getByRole('textbox', { name: 'Edit title' }), { key: 'Enter' })
157	    await waitFor(() => expect(onUpdate).toHaveBeenCalledWith('1', { title: 'New title' }))
158	    await waitFor(() => expect(screen.queryByRole('textbox', { name: 'Edit title' })).not.toBeInTheDocument())
159	  })
160	
161	  it('pressing Enter on assignee input calls onUpdate(id, { assignee }) and exits edit mode', async () => {
162	    const onUpdate = vi.fn().mockResolvedValue({})
163	    render(<CardTile card={card} onCardClick={vi.fn()} onUpdate={onUpdate} />)
164	    fireEvent.click(screen.getByText('Alice'))
165	    fireEvent.change(screen.getByRole('textbox', { name: 'Edit assignee' }), { target: { value: 'Bob' } })
166	    fireEvent.keyDown(screen.getByRole('textbox', { name: 'Edit assignee' }), { key: 'Enter' })
167	    await waitFor(() => expect(onUpdate).toHaveBeenCalledWith('1', { assignee: 'Bob' }))
168	    await waitFor(() => expect(screen.queryByRole('textbox', { name: 'Edit assignee' })).not.toBeInTheDocument())
169	  })
170	
171	  it('pressing Enter with empty assignee calls onUpdate(id, { assignee: null })', async () => {
172	    const onUpdate = vi.fn().mockResolvedValue({})
173	    render(<CardTile card={card} onCardClick={vi.fn()} onUpdate={onUpdate} />)
174	    fireEvent.click(screen.getByText('Alice'))
175	    fireEvent.change(screen.getByRole('textbox', { name: 'Edit assignee' }), { target: { value: '' } })
176	    fireEvent.keyDown(screen.getByRole('textbox', { name: 'Edit assignee' }), { key: 'Enter' })
177	    await waitFor(() => expect(onUpdate).toHaveBeenCalledWith('1', { assignee: null }))
178	  })
179	
180	  // Escape key cancels
181	  it('pressing Escape on title input cancels edit without calling onUpdate', () => {
182	    const onUpdate = vi.fn()
183	    render(<CardTile card={card} onCardClick={vi.fn()} onUpdate={onUpdate} />)
184	    fireEvent.click(screen.getByRole('heading', { name: 'Fix bug' }))
185	    fireEvent.keyDown(screen.getByRole('textbox', { name: 'Edit title' }), { key: 'Escape' })
186	    expect(onUpdate).not.toHaveBeenCalled()
187	  })
188	
189	  it('pressing Escape on title input restores original title text', () => {
190	    render(<CardTile card={card} onCardClick={vi.fn()} onUpdate={vi.fn()} />)
191	    fireEvent.click(screen.getByRole('heading', { name: 'Fix bug' }))
192	    fireEvent.change(screen.getByRole('textbox', { name: 'Edit title' }), { target: { value: 'Changed title' } })
193	    fireEvent.keyDown(screen.getByRole('textbox', { name: 'Edit title' }), { key: 'Escape' })
194	    expect(screen.getByRole('heading', { name: 'Fix bug' })).toBeInTheDocument()
195	  })
196	
197	  it('pressing Escape on assignee input cancels edit without calling onUpdate', () => {
198	    const onUpdate = vi.fn()
199	    render(<CardTile card={card} onCardClick={vi.fn()} onUpdate={onUpdate} />)
200	    fireEvent.click(screen.getByText('Alice'))
201	    fireEvent.keyDown(screen.getByRole('textbox', { name: 'Edit assignee' }), { key: 'Escape' })
202	    expect(onUpdate).not.toHaveBeenCalled()
203	  })
204	
205	  it('pressing Escape on assignee input restores original assignee text', () => {
206	    render(<CardTile card={card} onCardClick={vi.fn()} onUpdate={vi.fn()} />)
207	    fireEvent.click(screen.getByText('Alice'))
208	    fireEvent.change(screen.getByRole('textbox', { name: 'Edit assignee' }), { target: { value: 'Changed' } })
209	    fireEvent.keyDown(screen.getByRole('textbox', { name: 'Edit assignee' }), { key: 'Escape' })
210	    expect(screen.getByText('Alice')).toBeInTheDocument()
211	  })
212	
213	  // Blur saves
214	  it('blurring title input calls onUpdate(id, { title })', async () => {
215	    const onUpdate = vi.fn().mockResolvedValue({})
216	    render(<CardTile card={card} onCardClick={vi.fn()} onUpdate={onUpdate} />)
217	    fireEvent.click(screen.getByRole('heading', { name: 'Fix bug' }))
218	    fireEvent.change(screen.getByRole('textbox', { name: 'Edit title' }), { target: { value: 'Blurred title' } })
219	    fireEvent.blur(screen.getByRole('textbox', { name: 'Edit title' }))
220	    await waitFor(() => expect(onUpdate).toHaveBeenCalledWith('1', { title: 'Blurred title' }))
221	  })
222	
223	  it('blurring assignee input calls onUpdate(id, { assignee })', async () => {
224	    const onUpdate = vi.fn().mockResolvedValue({})
225	    render(<CardTile card={card} onCardClick={vi.fn()} onUpdate={onUpdate} />)
226	    fireEvent.click(screen.getByText('Alice'))
227	    fireEvent.change(screen.getByRole('textbox', { name: 'Edit assignee' }), { target: { value: 'Bob' } })
228	    fireEvent.blur(screen.getByRole('textbox', { name: 'Edit assignee' }))
229	    await waitFor(() => expect(onUpdate).toHaveBeenCalledWith('1', { assignee: 'Bob' }))
230	  })
231	
232	  it('blurring assignee input with empty value calls onUpdate(id, { assignee: null })', async () => {
233	    const onUpdate = vi.fn().mockResolvedValue({})
234	    render(<CardTile card={card} onCardClick={vi.fn()} onUpdate={onUpdate} />)
235	    fireEvent.click(screen.getByText('Alice'))
236	    fireEvent.change(screen.getByRole('textbox', { name: 'Edit assignee' }), { target: { value: '' } })
237	    fireEvent.blur(screen.getByRole('textbox', { name: 'Edit assignee' }))
238	    await waitFor(() => expect(onUpdate).toHaveBeenCalledWith('1', { assignee: null }))
239	  })
240	
241	  // Validation
242	  it('empty title shows validation error and does not call onUpdate', () => {
243	    const onUpdate = vi.fn()
244	    render(<CardTile card={card} onCardClick={vi.fn()} onUpdate={onUpdate} />)
245	    fireEvent.click(screen.getByRole('heading', { name: 'Fix bug' }))
246	    fireEvent.change(screen.getByRole('textbox', { name: 'Edit title' }), { target: { value: '' } })
247	    fireEvent.keyDown(screen.getByRole('textbox', { name: 'Edit title' }), { key: 'Enter' })
248	    expect(screen.getByRole('alert')).toHaveTextContent('Title is required')
249	    expect(onUpdate).not.toHaveBeenCalled()
250	  })
251	
252	  it('whitespace-only title shows validation error and does not call onUpdate', () => {
253	    const onUpdate = vi.fn()
254	    render(<CardTile card={card} onCardClick={vi.fn()} onUpdate={onUpdate} />)
255	    fireEvent.click(screen.getByRole('heading', { name: 'Fix bug' }))
256	    fireEvent.change(screen.getByRole('textbox', { name: 'Edit title' }), { target: { value: '   ' } })
257	    fireEvent.keyDown(screen.getByRole('textbox', { name: 'Edit title' }), { key: 'Enter' })
258	    expect(screen.getByRole('alert')).toHaveTextContent('Title is required')
259	    expect(onUpdate).not.toHaveBeenCalled()
260	  })
261	
262	  // Error handling and rollback
263	  it('onUpdate failure on title save stays in edit mode with typed value preserved', async () => {
264	    const onUpdate = vi.fn().mockRejectedValue(new Error('Server error'))
265	    render(<CardTile card={card} onCardClick={vi.fn()} onUpdate={onUpdate} />)
266	    fireEvent.click(screen.getByRole('heading', { name: 'Fix bug' }))
267	    fireEvent.change(screen.getByRole('textbox', { name: 'Edit title' }), { target: { value: 'New title' } })
268	    fireEvent.keyDown(screen.getByRole('textbox', { name: 'Edit title' }), { key: 'Enter' })
269	    await waitFor(() => expect(screen.getByRole('alert')).toHaveTextContent('Server error'))
270	    expect(screen.getByRole('textbox', { name: 'Edit title' })).toHaveValue('New title')
271	  })
272	
273	  it('onUpdate failure on title save shows an error alert', async () => {
274	    const onUpdate = vi.fn().mockRejectedValue(new Error('Server error'))
275	    render(<CardTile card={card} onCardClick={vi.fn()} onUpdate={onUpdate} />)
276	    fireEvent.click(screen.getByRole('heading', { name: 'Fix bug' }))
277	    fireEvent.keyDown(screen.getByRole('textbox', { name: 'Edit title' }), { key: 'Enter' })
278	    await waitFor(() => expect(screen.getByRole('alert')).toHaveTextContent('Server error'))
279	  })
280	
281	  it('onUpdate failure on assignee save stays in edit mode and shows an error alert', async () => {
282	    const onUpdate = vi.fn().mockRejectedValue(new Error('Assignee error'))
283	    render(<CardTile card={card} onCardClick={vi.fn()} onUpdate={onUpdate} />)
284	    fireEvent.click(screen.getByText('Alice'))
285	    fireEvent.keyDown(screen.getByRole('textbox', { name: 'Edit assignee' }), { key: 'Enter' })
286	    await waitFor(() => expect(screen.getByRole('alert')).toHaveTextContent('Assignee error'))
287	    expect(screen.getByRole('textbox', { name: 'Edit assignee' })).toBeInTheDocument()
288	  })

Exit code 0. ``` > kanban-client@1.0.0 lint > eslint src ``` --- ### 3.2 Client Tests — `npm -w client run test` **Result: ✅ PASS** All 11 test suites pass, 402 individual tests pass. ``` ✓ src/api/client.test.js (27 tests) 7ms ✓ src/hooks/useWebSocket.test.js (42 tests) 90ms ✓ src/components/CardModal/CommentList.test.jsx (32 tests) 260ms ✓ src/components/CreateCardForm.test.jsx (33 tests) 320ms ✓ src/components/Board/CardTile.test.jsx (38 tests) 432ms ✓ src/components/CardModal/BlockEditor.test.jsx (32 tests) 292ms ✓ src/components/Board/Board.test.jsx (17 tests) 277ms ✓ src/components/Board/Column.test.jsx (8 tests) 63ms ✓ src/components/Board/CardModal.test.jsx (68 tests) 687ms ✓ src/App.test.jsx (4 tests) 39ms ✓ src/hooks/useBoard.test.js (101 tests) 4954ms Test Files 11 passed (11) Tests 402 passed (402) Duration 5.80s ``` Note: Several `act(...)` warnings appear in stderr for `useBoard.test.js` — these are pre-existing warnings from the WebSocket integration tests (unrelated to task 22) and do not cause test failures. **Task-22-specific suites:** | Suite | Tests | Status | |-------|-------|--------| | `CardTile.test.jsx` | 38 (37 new inline-editing + 1 pre-existing) | ✅ All pass | | `CardModal.test.jsx` | 68 | ✅ All pass | --- ### 3.3 Client Build — `npm -w client run build` **Result: ✅ PASS** ``` > kanban-client@1.0.0 build > vite build vite v5.4.21 building for production... transforming... ✓ 558 modules transformed. rendering chunks... computing gzip size... dist/index.html 0.48 kB │ gzip: 0.30 kB dist/assets/index-Dbm5wWgb.css 30.18 kB │ gzip: 6.08 kB dist/assets/module-BvCTiNll.js 77.23 kB │ gzip: 27.78 kB dist/assets/native-B5Vb9Oiz.js 380.35 kB │ gzip: 82.06 kB dist/assets/index-CYxwf42u.js 1,356.65 kB │ gzip: 419.35 kB (!) Some chunks are larger than 500 kB after minification. ✓ built in 1.46s ``` The chunk-size warning (`index-CYxwf42u.js` is 1,356 kB unminified) is a pre-existing advisory from the `@blocknote` rich-text editor dependency introduced in task 14. It does not fail the build. --- ### 3.4 Setup/Integration Tests — `npm run test:setup` **Result: ✅ PASS** ``` # tests 76 # suites 13 # pass 76 # fail 0 # duration_ms 60ms ``` All 76 structural/integration tests pass (package.json shape, Docker config, file structure, proxy settings, etc.). --- ### 3.5 Server Tests — `npm run test:server` **Result: ❌ FAIL (pre-existing — not caused by task 22)** ``` # tests 25 # pass 21 # fail 4 ``` Failing suites: | Suite | Error | |-------|-------| | `test/cards.test.mjs` | `ERR_MODULE_NOT_FOUND: Cannot find package 'express'` | | `test/comments.test.mjs` | `ERR_MODULE_NOT_FOUND: Cannot find package 'express'` | | `test/db.test.mjs` | `ERR_MODULE_NOT_FOUND: Cannot find package 'better-sqlite3'` | | `test/server.test.mjs` | `ERR_MODULE_NOT_FOUND: Cannot find package 'express'` | The WebSocket-only suite (`ws.test.mjs`) passes with 21 tests as it does not depend on `express` or `better-sqlite3`. **Root cause:** The server workspace `node_modules` directory (`kanban/server/node_modules/`) does not exist — only the root workspace `node_modules` is present. The server's `express`, `better-sqlite3`, `cors`, and `uuid` packages are not available as a result. **Confirmed pre-existing:** This failure is present on the commit before task 22 (`f0bd015` — Docker configuration). Task 22 touches no server code and introduced no server changes. This is an environment/installation issue unrelated to this task. --- ## 4. Implementation Review ### 4.1 CardTile — Inline Editing **File:** `kanban/client/src/components/Board/CardTile.jsx` The implementation correctly satisfies all sub-task requirements: - **Click-to-edit:** Clicking the `

` title or `

` assignee element replaces the text with an ``. `stopPropagation()` prevents the card-level `onCardClick` from firing. - **State management:** Separate `isEditingTitle` / `isEditingAssignee` boolean states. Mutual exclusivity enforced: opening one field closes the other and resets its value. - **Visual feedback:** `card-tile-editing` CSS class applied to the tile wrapper when either field is in edit mode. Input fields styled with blue border (`border: 1px solid #4a6fa5`). - **Focus management:** `useEffect` watches `isEditingTitle` / `isEditingAssignee` and calls `.focus()` on the respective `ref` when entering edit mode. - **Enter saves / Escape cancels:** `onKeyDown` handler sets `skipBlurRef.current = true` before the async call (preventing double-save when blur fires after Enter), then invokes the save or cancel path. - **Blur auto-saves:** `onBlur` handler checks `skipBlurRef` before calling the save handler. - **Validation:** `editTitle.trim() === ''` check returns early with a "Title is required" error displayed in `role="alert"`. Assignee can be emptied (mapped to `null`). - **Error handling:** `try-catch` around `onUpdate()` call. Error message shown in `role="alert"`. On failure, the component stays in edit mode with the typed value preserved (tested at line 263–271). - **Loading state:** `isSaving` state triggers a `Saving…` indicator. - **Rollback:** On Escape, `setEditTitle(card.title)` / `setEditAssignee(card.assignee ?? '')` restores original values. - **Separate blur refs:** `skipTitleBlurRef` and `skipAssigneeBlurRef` are correctly kept as separate refs (not shared), avoiding interference between the two fields. No issues found in `CardTile`. --- ### 4.2 CardModal — Inline Editing **File:** `kanban/client/src/components/Board/CardModal.jsx` The implementation correctly satisfies all sub-task requirements: - **Edit button pattern:** "Edit" button next to the static title/assignee display triggers edit mode. Save and Cancel buttons are shown while editing. - **Focus management:** `useEffect` on `isEditingTitle` / `isEditingAssignee` calls `.focus()` on the respective input ref. - **Enter saves / blur auto-saves:** `onKeyDown` sets `skipTitleBlurRef.current = true` before calling `handleSaveTitle()`; `onBlur` checks the flag before saving. - **Save / Cancel double-save prevention:** Both Save and Cancel buttons use `onMouseDown` to set `skipBlurRef.current = true`, preventing the blur handler from triggering a second save or unwanted save on cancel. - **Escape key:** A `document.addEventListener('keydown', ...)` `useEffect` (re-registered whenever editing state changes) intercepts Escape: cancels active field edit (instead of closing modal), or closes modal if no field is being edited and the block editor is not active. - **Block editor awareness:** `onEditingChange={setIsEditingDescription}` is passed to `BlockEditor` so that Escape does not close the modal while the block editor is in its own edit mode. - **Validation:** Same `trim() === ''` check as `CardTile`. Error displayed in `role="alert"`. - **Error handling / loading states:** `try-catch` around `onUpdate()`. On failure: stays in edit mode with typed value preserved (consistent retry UX). On success: exits edit mode. - **Mutual exclusivity:** Opening title edit calls `setIsEditingAssignee(false)` and vice versa. No issues found in `CardModal`. --- ### 4.3 Column / Board Wiring `Column.jsx` correctly forwards `onUpdate` to each `CardTile`. `Board.jsx` now passes `onUpdate={updateCard}` to all three `Column` instances (Ready, In Progress, Done). Previously, In Progress and Done columns did not receive this prop, which would have silently swallowed update attempts. --- ### 4.4 Test Coverage Task 22 added the following test coverage: **CardTile.test.jsx** (38 total, 37 new): - Edit mode entry for title and assignee (renders input, stops propagation, auto-focuses) - Clicking assignee with `null` value starts with empty input - Clicking card body while editing does not call `onCardClick` - Mutual exclusivity between title and assignee fields - Enter key saves for title and assignee (including empty assignee → `null`) - Escape key cancels and restores original values - Blur auto-saves for title and assignee (including empty → `null`) - Empty/whitespace title validation shows error and blocks `onUpdate` - API error stays in edit mode, preserves typed value, shows alert - `card-tile-editing` CSS class applied during edit mode - `Saving…` indicator while save is in-flight **CardModal.test.jsx** (68 total): - Pre-existing: portal rendering, close button, Escape closes modal, overlay click, dialog role - Edit title: button visibility, input value, heading hidden, focus, Save/Cancel buttons shown - Cancel restores original title, Escape cancels title edit without closing modal - Save: calls `onUpdate`, loading state, exits on success, shows error on rejection, stays in edit mode on rejection - Empty title validation - Enter key triggers save - Mutual exclusivity (opening title cancels assignee edit and vice versa) - Blur auto-saves; blur suppressed when Cancel clicked; no double-save when Save clicked - Symmetrical coverage for assignee field - Delete confirmation flow tested --- ## 5. Behavioral Notes ### 5.1 On-failure behavior consistency On a title save failure in `CardTile`, the component stays in edit mode with the typed value preserved (the test at line 263 asserts `toHaveValue('New title')` after a rejection). This matches the `CardModal` behavior — both surfaces preserve the typed value for retry. This is consistent and correct. ### 5.2 Separate blur refs `CardTile` uses separate `skipTitleBlurRef` and `skipAssigneeBlurRef` refs (one per field), which is correct and avoids any cross-field interference. `CardModal` also uses separate `skipTitleBlurRef` and `skipAssigneeBlurRef` refs for the same reason. --- ## 6. Overall Assessment | Category | Status | Notes | |----------|--------|-------| | ESLint | ✅ PASS | No errors or warnings | | Client unit tests | ✅ PASS | 402/402 tests across 11 suites | | Client build | ✅ PASS | Bundles successfully (chunk-size advisory is pre-existing) | | Setup/integration tests | ✅ PASS | 76/76 pass | | Server unit tests | ❌ FAIL (pre-existing) | `express`/`better-sqlite3` not in server `node_modules`; not caused by task 22 | | Click-to-edit (CardTile) | ✅ Correct | All required behaviors implemented and tested | | Click-to-edit (CardModal) | ✅ Correct | All required behaviors implemented and tested | | Keyboard shortcuts (Enter/Escape/blur) | ✅ Correct | Implemented in both components | | Validation (empty title) | ✅ Correct | `trim() === ''` check in both components | | Error handling + error display | ✅ Correct | `try-catch` + `role="alert"` in both components | | Loading state | ✅ Correct | `isSaving` + "Saving…" indicator in both components | | Rollback on Escape | ✅ Correct | Restores original value in both components | | Error-path behavior (API failure) | ✅ Correct | Stays in edit mode, preserves typed value in both components | | Visual feedback (CSS class) | ✅ Correct | `card-tile-editing` class applied during tile edit mode | | Board/Column wiring | ✅ Correct | `onUpdate` forwarded to all three columns and all card tiles | **The task 22 implementation is functionally correct and fully tested.** All client-side checks pass. The only failing check (`npm run test:server`) is a pre-existing environment issue (server `node_modules` not installed) that predates task 22 and is unrelated to the inline editing feature.

+- Save: calls `onUpdate`, loading state, exits on success, shows error on rejection, stays in edit mode on rejection
+- Mutual exclusivity (opening title cancels assignee edit and vice versa)
+- Blur auto-saves; blur suppressed when Cancel clicked; no double-save when Save clicked

307	    fireEvent.keyDown(screen.getByRole('textbox', { name: 'Edit title' }), { key: 'Enter' })
308	    await waitFor(() => expect(screen.getByLabelText('Saving')).toBeInTheDocument())
309	  })
310	})

` title or `

` assignee replaces the element with an ``. `e.stopPropagation()` on both handlers prevents the card-level `onCardClick` from firing. - **Mutual exclusivity:** Opening title edit closes assignee edit and vice versa, resetting the alternate field's value. - **Focus management:** Two `useEffect` hooks trigger `.focus()` on the appropriate input ref when edit mode is entered. - **Enter saves / Escape cancels:** `onKeyDown` sets `skipBlurRef.current = true` before calling save/cancel to prevent the subsequent `onBlur` from triggering a duplicate save. - **Blur auto-saves:** `onBlur` checks the skip-blur ref before invoking the save handler. - **Separate skip-blur refs:** `skipTitleBlurRef` and `skipAssigneeBlurRef` are kept separate — no cross-field interference. - **Validation:** `editTitle.trim() === ''` blocks the API call and displays `role="alert"` error. Empty assignee is mapped to `null`. - **Error handling:** `try-catch` wraps `onUpdate()`. On failure: edit mode is preserved with the typed value intact, and the error message is displayed in `role="alert"`. - **Loading state:** `isSaving` toggles a `Saving…` indicator. - **Visual feedback:** `card-tile-editing` CSS class is applied to the tile wrapper when either field is active. ### 4.2 CardModal — Inline Editing (`CardModal.jsx`) All task requirements are correctly implemented: - **Edit button pattern:** Static title/assignee display includes an "Edit" button. Clicking it activates the corresponding input with Save and Cancel buttons. - **Focus management:** Same `useEffect` pattern as CardTile. - **Double-save prevention:** Save and Cancel buttons use `onMouseDown` to set `skipBlurRef.current = true` before the input's `onBlur` fires, preventing an unwanted auto-save. - **Escape key:** A `document.addEventListener` effect intercepts Escape: cancels the active field edit (without closing the modal), or falls through to `onClose()` when no field is being edited and the block editor is idle. - **Block editor awareness:** `onEditingChange={setIsEditingDescription}` is passed to `BlockEditor` so that Escape does not close the modal while the block editor has an active edit session. - **Validation and error handling:** Same `trim() === ''` check and `role="alert"` pattern as CardTile. On API failure, edit mode is preserved with the typed value intact for retry. - **Loading state:** Save button text changes to "Saving…" and is disabled while `isSaving` is true. - **Mutual exclusivity:** Handled symmetrically — opening one field closes the other. ### 4.3 Column and Board Wiring `Column.jsx` correctly accepts and forwards `onUpdate` to each `CardTile`. `Board.jsx` now passes `onUpdate={updateCard}` to all three `Column` instances (Ready, In Progress, Done). Previously, In Progress and Done columns did not pass this prop; without it, CardTile's save handlers would silently throw because `onUpdate` was `undefined`. --- ## 5. The task 22 implementation is functionally correct, fully tested, and introduces no regressions.**

-- **Click-to-edit:** Clicking the `

` title or `

` assignee element replaces the text with an ``. `stopPropagation()` prevents the card-level `onCardClick` from firing.
-- **State management:** Separate `isEditingTitle` / `isEditingAssignee` boolean states. Mutual exclusivity enforced: opening one field closes the other and resets its value.
-- **Visual feedback:** `card-tile-editing` CSS class applied to the tile wrapper when either field is in edit mode. Input fields styled with blue border (`border: 1px solid #4a6fa5`).
-- **Focus management:** `useEffect` watches `isEditingTitle` / `isEditingAssignee` and calls `.focus()` on the respective `ref` when entering edit mode.

-- **Enter saves / Escape cancels:** `onKeyDown` handler sets `skipBlurRef.current = true` before the async call (preventing double-save when blur fires after Enter), then invokes the save or cancel path.
-- **Blur auto-saves:** `onBlur` handler checks `skipBlurRef` before calling the save handler.
-- **Validation:** `editTitle.trim() === ''` check returns early with a "Title is required" error displayed in `role="alert"`. Assignee can be emptied (mapped to `null`).

-- **Error handling:** `try-catch` around `onUpdate()` call. Error message shown in `role="alert"`. On failure, the component stays in edit mode with the typed value preserved (tested at line 263–271).
-- **Loading state:** `isSaving` state triggers a `Saving…` indicator.
-- **Separate blur refs:** `skipTitleBlurRef` and `skipAssigneeBlurRef` are correctly kept as separate refs (not shared), avoiding interference between the two fields.
+- **Edit button pattern:** Static title/assignee display includes an "Edit" button. Clicking it activates the corresponding input with Save and Cancel buttons.
+- **Double-save prevention:** Save and Cancel buttons use `onMouseDown` to set `skipBlurRef.current = true` before the input's `onBlur` fires, preventing an unwanted auto-save.

+- **Escape key:** A `document.addEventListener` effect intercepts Escape: cancels the active field edit (without closing the modal), or falls through to `onClose()` when no field is being edited and the block editor is idle.
+- **Block editor awareness:** `onEditingChange={setIsEditingDescription}` is passed to `BlockEditor` so that Escape does not close the modal while the block editor has an active edit session.
+- **Validation and error handling:** Same `trim() === ''` check and `role="alert"` pattern as CardTile. On API failure, edit mode is preserved with the typed value intact for retry.
+- **Loading state:** Save button text changes to "Saving…" and is disabled while `isSaving` is true.
+- **Mutual exclusivity:** Handled symmetrically — opening one field closes the other.

+`Column.jsx` correctly accepts and forwards `onUpdate` to each `CardTile`. `Board.jsx` now passes `onUpdate={updateCard}` to all three `Column` instances (Ready, In Progress, Done). Previously, In Progress and Done columns did not pass this prop; without it, CardTile's save handlers would silently throw because `onUpdate` was `undefined`.
-The implementation correctly satisfies all sub-task requirements:
-- **Edit button pattern:** "Edit" button next to the static title/assignee display triggers edit mode. Save and Cancel buttons are shown while editing.
-- **Focus management:** `useEffect` on `isEditingTitle` / `isEditingAssignee` calls `.focus()` on the respective input ref.
-- **Enter saves / blur auto-saves:** `onKeyDown` sets `skipTitleBlurRef.current = true` before calling `handleSaveTitle()`; `onBlur` checks the flag before saving.

-- **Save / Cancel double-save prevention:** Both Save and Cancel buttons use `onMouseDown` to set `skipBlurRef.current = true`, preventing the blur handler from triggering a second save or unwanted save on cancel.

-- **Escape key:** A `document.addEventListener('keydown', ...)` `useEffect` (re-registered whenever editing state changes) intercepts Escape: cancels active field edit (instead of closing modal), or closes modal if no field is being edited and the block editor is not active.
-- **Block editor awareness:** `onEditingChange={setIsEditingDescription}` is passed to `BlockEditor` so that Escape does not close the modal while the block editor is in its own edit mode.
-- **Validation:** Same `trim() === ''` check as `CardTile`. Error displayed in `role="alert"`.
-- **Error handling / loading states:** `try-catch` around `onUpdate()`. On failure: stays in edit mode with typed value preserved (consistent retry UX). On success: exits edit mode.
-- **Mutual exclusivity:** Opening title edit calls `setIsEditingAssignee(false)` and vice versa.

-`Column.jsx` correctly forwards `onUpdate` to each `CardTile`. `Board.jsx` now passes `onUpdate={updateCard}` to all three `Column` instances (Ready, In Progress, Done). Previously, In Progress and Done columns did not receive this prop, which would have silently swallowed update attempts.
-- Edit mode entry for title and assignee (renders input, stops propagation, auto-focuses)
-- Clicking assignee with `null` value starts with empty input
-- Clicking card body while editing does not call `onCardClick`
-- Mutual exclusivity between title and assignee fields
-- Enter key saves for title and assignee (including empty assignee → `null`)
-- Escape key cancels and restores original values
-- Blur auto-saves for title and assignee (including empty → `null`)
-- Empty/whitespace title validation shows error and blocks `onUpdate`
-- API error stays in edit mode, preserves typed value, shows alert
-- `card-tile-editing` CSS class applied during edit mode
-- Pre-existing: portal rendering, close button, Escape closes modal, overlay click, dialog role
-- Edit title: button visibility, input value, heading hidden, focus, Save/Cancel buttons shown
-- Cancel restores original title, Escape cancels title edit without closing modal
-- Save: calls `onUpdate`, loading state, exits on success, shows error on rejection, stays in edit mode on rejection
-- Mutual exclusivity (opening title cancels assignee edit and vice versa)
-- Blur auto-saves; blur suppressed when Cancel clicked; no double-save when Save clicked

-On a title save failure in `CardTile`, the component stays in edit mode with the typed value preserved (the test at line 263 asserts `toHaveValue('New title')` after a rejection). This matches the `CardModal` behavior — both surfaces preserve the typed value for retry. This is consistent and correct.

-`CardTile` uses separate `skipTitleBlurRef` and `skipAssigneeBlurRef` refs (one per field), which is correct and avoids any cross-field interference. `CardModal` also uses separate `skipTitleBlurRef` and `skipAssigneeBlurRef` refs for the same reason.
